L1.head: Evolutionary algorithms
L1.claim: Evolutionary algorithms enable multi-objective optimization in complex spaces

L1.head: Microfluidic reactors
L1.claim: Microfluidic reactors enable rapid iteration of reaction conditions

L1.head: Integration of evolutionary algorithms and microfluidic reactors
L1.claim: Integration enables emergent discovery of nanoparticle conditions with Pareto-optimal optical tradeoffs

L1.head: emergent discovery of Pareto-optimal conditions
L1.claim: Emergent discovery leads to nanoparticles with simultaneous optimization of plasmon resonance wavelength and FWHM

L2.head: Chemical spaces
L2.claim: Chemical spaces have intrinsic lower-dimensional structure

L2.head: Topological data analysis
L2.claim: Topological methods identify persistent homology features in high-dimensional data

L2.head: Microfluidic reactors
L2.claim: Microfluidics generate data suitable for topological analysis

L2.head: persistent homology navigation
L2.claim: Navigation guided by persistent homology requires fewer iterations to reach target nanoparticle properties

L2.head: Nanoparticle formation
L2.claim: Nanoparticle formation involves non-equilibrium kinetic pathways

L2.head: Reinforcement learning
L2.claim: Reinforcement learning excels at discovering non-obvious temporal strategies

L2.head: RL agents
L2.claim: RL agents manipulate reagent timing to trap metastable states

L2.head: RL-designed protocols
L2.claim: RL-designed protocols produce nanoparticle shapes not found in conventional synthesis databases

L3.head: Microfluidic mixing
L3.claim: Microfluidic mixing affects nanoparticle formation kinetics

L3.head: Standard Bayesian optimization kernels
L3.claim: Standard BO kernels ignore transport physics in microfluidics

L3.head: Physics-informed kernels
L3.claim: Physics-informed kernels better model synthesis space by incorporating fluid dynamics

L3.head: Physics-informed Bayesian optimization
L3.claim: Physics-informed BO shows faster convergence for nanoparticles in complex flow regimes

L3.head: Reagent molecular structure
L3.claim: Reagent molecular structure influences nanoparticle growth

L3.head: Graph neural networks
L3.claim: GNNs process molecular graph representations effectively

L3.head: Combined spectral and molecular features
L3.claim: Combined features capture fundamental molecular-structure-to-nanostructure relationships

L3.head: GNN models
L3.claim: GNN models show better generalization to unseen reagent classes
